text: Review handoff should keep explicit queue ownership for the next reviewer.
SQLite WAL mode should stay enabled so readers can query during writes.
Context compaction should write a bridge note before details are lost.
Store machine-readable, token-efficient records instead of narrative memory.
Wrong DB splits break trust in recall and need one canonical runtime path.
High reasoning should stay on planning and validation work instead of bounded code edits.
Cross-project gotchas should be recalled before external search so later projects reuse prior fixes.
Single ownership keeps subagent execution from drifting when the contract is still moving.
Punctuation-heavy values.yaml queries need a safe FTS fallback instead of naive token parsing.
Check local bridge memory before external search for issue-like prompts.
Release cutover handoff should be claimed by one worker and acknowledged after the checklist is complete.
Keep one canonical bridge database path so the watcher and MCP server read the same database and logs.
Domain notes should synthesize repeated learn records instead of storing one more noisy summary.
Use higher reasoning for architecture review and validation, not for bounded code edits.
Review queue ownership should survive claim and extend so the next reviewer sees who holds the work.
If FTS tokenization breaks on punctuation, fall back to substring recall before declaring a miss.
